keyRules: A maximum of 120 m above ground level (EASA Open category)
Registration as a UAS operator with the Transport Authority for drones weighing 250 g or more or equipped with a camera
Required A1/A3 online training — minimum age 16

Minimum distances by subcategory: A1 — do not fly over bystanders; A2 — at least 30 m from people (5 m in low-speed mode); A3 — at least 150 m from residential, commercial, industrial, and recreational areas.
Always maintain visual line of sight (VLOS) with the drone
Never fly over people, crowds, or public events

M. R. Štefánik Airport, Bratislava: complete ban on drone flights within a 5-km radius up to an altitude of 500 m (PUAS0103M, effective December 6, 2025). Košice, Poprad, Žilina, and Piešťany Airports (effective March 31, 2026): within 5 km of the airport, flights above 30 m are permitted only after coordination—via email to the air traffic control unit at least 24 hours in advance.
Night flights are permitted—the drone must have a green flashing light turned on.
National Parks (Tatra Mountains, Slovak Paradise, Low Tatras): Restrictions are in effect
Bratislava Castle and Government Areas: No-Fly Zones
Before each flight, check the UAS geographic areas (PUAS/RUAS zones)—the list is maintained by the Ministry of Transportation and the Transportation Authority, and the zones are subject to change.
The Malacky-Kuchyňa, Sliač, and Prešov military airfields (RUAS zones): flights are permitted only with the approval of the Ministry of Defense of the Slovak Republic.
importantNotes: Distances from people are governed by EASA subcategories (A1/A2/A3)—the old national rule of 50 meters no longer applies.
Registration of UAS operators is handled by the Transport Authority — valid throughout the EU/EASA
The Tatra Mountains and national parks have significant restrictions
The area surrounding M. R. Štefánik Airport: a complete ban on drones within a 5-km radius (PUAS0103M) — this also covers a large part of Bratislava.
The GDPR applies to the capture of identifiable images

fields.disclaimerText: Before flying, always check the official sources of the Civil Aviation Authority and the LPS airspace map. This app is a tool—not a legal authority. Slovakia follows EASA regulations with national supplements.
